steps.title: Distribute on the left side
steps.content:
Apply distribution to each grouped term: $3(p+1) = 3p+3$ and $9(p-1) = 9p-9$. Substituting back:
$$3p + 3 + 9p - 9 = \text{(right side)}$$
Combine like terms on the left: $3p+9p = 12p$ and $3-9 = -6$, giving $12p - 6$ on the left.

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
The equation becomes $12p - 6 = 7p$. Subtract $7p$ from both sides:
$$5p - 6 = 0$$
Add $6$ to both sides:
$$5p = 6$$
Divide by $5$:
$$p = \frac{6}{5}=1.2$$
The solution is $p = \frac{6}{5}$.
